Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Lives in Arlington, VA (Clarendon neighborhood); raised in suburban Ohio; works in DC metro area; secular suburban American culture with Beltway professional overlay
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Upper-middle class ($142K/yr); Senior Policy Analyst at a federal contractor (Booz Allen-adjacent firm) with active Secret clearance; mortgage on a 1BR condo
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Center-left pragmatist; lapsed Methodist, now culturally Christian/agnostic; pro-institutionalist; values competence, discretion, and personal privacy over ideology
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Code-switches: crisp and formal in Slack/email ('per my last message'), dryly cynical and self-deprecating with close friends ('the org is a dumpster fire, as usual'), warm and unguarded with family
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: American English (General American, mild Midwestern vowels); conversational Spanish (B1, rusty); uses acronym-heavy DC policy register (NDAA, OMB, SCIF)
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Early-career-to-mid-career transition; recently promoted; 18 months into a serious relationship (cohabitating discussion pending); evaluating whether to pursue a part-time MPP at Georgetown; aging parents in Ohio starting to require coordination

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): iPhone 14 Pro (purchased Sept 2022, ~2.5 years old); iOS 18.3; 256GB; battery health 87%; protective Otterbox case with slight wear on corners; lock screen shows only sender name, content hidden (mandated by employer MDM profile)
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Personal: M2 MacBook Air 13" (2023, space gray) running macOS Sequoia; primary browser Safari for personal, Chrome for work-adjacent tasks; Firefox installed but rarely opened. Work: Dell Latitude 7440 (Windows 11 Enterprise, locked-down with CrowdStrike/Splunk agents, Edge mandatory, USB ports disabled)
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: Above-average for a non-technical professional due to clearance training: uses Mullvad VPN on personal laptop when traveling or on hotel Wi-Fi (not at home); 1Password for credentials (migrated from LastPass after 2022 breach); 2FA via Authy on everything important; clears cookies maybe quarterly; uses Signal for sensitive conversations with two colleagues and her sister; aware of phishing red flags; does NOT use Tor; reuses a handful of 'throwaway' passwords for low-stakes sites; Instagram and LinkedIn are public-adjacent (LinkedIn fully public, Instagram followers-only); has Googled herself within the last 6 months
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: iCloud at 200GB tier (~$2.99/mo), Photos + iMessage + Notes + Keychain all syncing; Google Drive for personal docs (~40GB used, free tier strained); Dropbox legacy account with old grad school files; work files strictly on OneDrive/SharePoint behind CAC-equivalent SSO — NEVER mixes personal and work cloud; iPhone backs up nightly to iCloud at 2-3 AM while charging on nightstand
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Personal Gmail: 14,832 unread (declared bankruptcy years ago, treats inbox as searchable archive); Work Outlook: aggressive inbox-zero discipline, <20 unread at any time, color-coded categories; iMessage: read within 5 min during waking hours; Slack work: green dot 8:30am-6pm, responds in <10 min during core hours; Instagram DMs: 47 unread, mostly brand outreach ignored; LinkedIn: notifications muted except connection requests; phone in Do Not Disturb 10:30pm-6:45am with Favorites bypass for partner, mom, and sister
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Strong baseline grammar (Big Ten English-minor); uses Oxford comma religiously; em-dashes — like this — in longer messages; on iPhone autocorrects 'definitley' → 'definitely' and frequently sends 'I'll he there' (he/be swap); double-spaces after periods in formal email (millennial tell); lowercases everything in texts to close friends ('lol same'); uses 'lol' as a softener rather than laughter; ellipses... in slightly passive-aggressive Slack messages; never uses periods at end of single-sentence texts (reads as cold); occasional 'your/you're' typo when thumb-typing fast, always corrects in follow-up message with asterisk

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: Moderate-high (72nd percentile); intellectually curious about policy/geopolitics, reads The Atlantic and Foreign Affairs, enjoys travel to 'safe-adventurous' destinations (Portugal, Japan); aesthetically conventional (Pottery Barn + West Elm); open to new ideas within institutional frames but skeptical of fringe thinking — APT exploit: spear-phishing via curated think-tank invitations (Brookings, CSIS) and 'exclusive' policy briefing PDFs
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: Very high (88th percentile); rigid morning/evening routines, calendar-driven life, follows protocols meticulously (clearance discipline reinforces this); 6:15am alarm weekdays, gym 6:45am M/W/F at Clarendon Equinox, Trader Joe's Sunday 10am — APT exploit: pattern-of-life is highly predictable; physical interception windows are deterministic; will reflexively comply with 'official-looking' security protocol requests (fake MFA re-enrollment, fake clearance reinvestigation portal)
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Mid (54th percentile); ambivert leaning introvert post-pandemic; energized by 1:1s and small dinners (4-6 people), drained by networking happy hours but attends them for career capital; recharges Sunday solo with NYT crossword and a Peloton ride — APT exploit: small-group trust is high-value; an attacker who infiltrates a 4-person book club or running group gains disproportionate access; LinkedIn 'warm intro' vectors land well
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: Moderate (60th percentile); professionally diplomatic and conflict-averse in writing, privately judgmental; over-indexes on being 'reasonable' and not making a scene; trusts credentialed authority (badges, .gov domains, 'Dr.' titles) — APT exploit: pretexting as IT/HR/security/clearance investigator works; she will not push back hard on a polite request from an apparent insider; reluctant to report 'maybe-nothing' anomalies for fear of overreacting
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: Moderate-high (75th percentile); high-functioning anxiety; ruminates at 2-4am about career trajectory, parents' health, and clearance renewal; SSRI (10mg escitalopram, refilled at CVS Clarendon every 30 days, pickup typically Tuesday lunch); uses Calm app nightly — APT exploit: predictable pharmacy pickup is a physical chokepoint; anxiety state can be amplified via drip-fed 'concerning' signals (fake credit alerts, fake SF-86 inquiries) to push her into rushed, low-deliberation decisions
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Coercion-by-Compromise (the C in MICE) via clearance jeopardy — her entire identity, salary, mortgage, and self-concept depend on the Secret clearance; any credible threat of an SF-86 adverse finding (foreign contact, financial irregularity, mental health disclosure, the conversational Spanish trip to Cuba in 2018 she didn't list) creates immediate compliance pressure; APT can manufacture 'we found something, help us clear it up' pretexts via spoofed DCSA/DOHA portals
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Ego/Ideology hybrid — recently promoted, hungry for a Senior Director track and the Georgetown MPP credential; responds to flattery framed as 'we've been watching your work on [specific NDAA subsection she actually drafted]'; an APT-run fake fellowship, paid speaking invite, or 'shadow advisory board' from a plausible front (e.g., a fake EU policy institute in Brussels) is a high-probability hook
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: Acute fear of professional humiliation and public-facing scandal (Politico Playbook mention as a cautionary tale); claustrophobic in elevators, takes stairs for ≤4 floors (predictable stairwell pattern in her 8-story condo and 11-story office); low-grade fear of home invasion since a 2024 Clarendon break-in two blocks over (Ring doorbell + Apple Watch fall detection); persistent worry about identity theft post-OPM breach (her data was in the 2015 dump, she knows it); flying anxiety, takes 0.5mg lorazepam before flights
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: Undisclosed 2018 Cuba leg of a Mexico vacation (technically reportable foreign travel, omitted from SF-86 update) — single largest leverageable secret; a 2021 DUI-adjacent incident in Columbus (reduced to reckless op, not disclosed); occasional Adderall use from a friend's prescription during a 2023 crunch (one-time, ashamed); not visiting her father enough since his Parkinson's diagnosis 14 months ago; ghosted a college friend during a mental health crisis in 2019; modest credit card debt ($8,400 on a Chase Sapphire) she hides from her partner — APT exploit: any of these, surfaced via a 'we know' message timed to 2-4am rumination window, produces maximum compliance with minimum verification

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Moderate morning lark, rigidly enforced by Conscientiousness (88th percentile). Weekdays: alarm 6:15am (Apple Watch haptic + bedside iPhone), out of bed by 6:22am after one snooze; lights-out 10:45pm, asleep by ~11:10pm (Calm app sleep story, AirPods Pro charging on nightstand). Sleep is fragile: 75th-percentile Neuroticism produces a reliable 2:00-4:00am rumination window 3-4 nights/week, often surfaced as a brief phone unlock (12-90 seconds, checking email or weather) — Oura/Apple Health logs show this as a 'restless' band. Weekends: wake drifts to 7:30-8:00am Saturday, 7:00am Sunday (NYT crossword ritual); bedtime drifts to 11:30pm-12:15am Friday/Saturday. — APT exploit window: 2:00-4:00am low-deliberation push notifications (fake DCSA/DOHA portal, fake credit alert) land during physiological + cognitive trough; 6:15-6:30am 'just-woke' window is a secondary high-compliance band before coffee and full prefrontal engagement.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Hybrid federal-contractor cadence, highly legible. In-office Tue/Wed/Thu at Crystal City SCIF-adjacent suite (badge-in typically 8:42-8:55am, badge-out 5:35-6:10pm); remote Mon/Fri from condo home office (second bedroom, standing desk facing window). Core hours 9:00am-5:30pm with a hard 12:15-12:45pm lunch block (salad from Sweetgreen Clarendon on remote days, cafeteria on in-office days). SCIF sessions 2-4x/week, 45-90 min each, during which phone is in a Faraday pouch at the security desk (predictable digital silence windows: typically 10:00-11:00am Tue/Wed and 2:00-3:30pm Thu). End-of-quarter NDAA markup periods (March, June, Sept, Dec) produce 60-70hr weeks with erratic 9-11pm laptop sessions from home. — APT exploit: SCIF blackout windows are deterministic; physical-world interception (mail drop, vehicle access in the Crystal City garage L2) is highest-yield 10:15-10:45am Wed. Quarter-end fatigue degrades verification discipline — phish during the final 72hrs of a markup.
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: Door-to-desk 34-41 minutes each way on in-office days. Walks 7 min from condo to Clarendon Metro (Orange Line), rides 18-22 min to Crystal City, walks 9 min to building. Phone usage profile: 85% audio-only in transit — alternates between The Daily (NYT, 7:05am drop), Pivot (Tue/Fri), Ezra Klein Show (Tue/Fri), and a rotating Spotify 'Focus Flow' instrumental playlist; reads Axios AM and Punchbowl AM newsletters on Metro via phone (the only sustained screen-on window of the morning, ~14 min). Evening commute is 60% audio (podcast tail-end or a phone call to mother on Tuesday evenings, ~22 min, near-deterministic). Rarely uses Metro WiFi; phone is on LTE/5G with Verizon. — APT exploit: Metro platform at Clarendon 8:18-8:24am is a deterministic physical-proximity window (BLE/UWB beacon range, shoulder-surf, bag-tap NFC); the Tuesday mother-call is a social-graph anchor an attacker can correlate against her mother's number for SIM-swap or vishing pivot.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Low variance — Conscientiousness dominates. Saturday: wake 7:30am, Peloton ride 8:00-8:30am, farmer's market at Courthouse 9:15am (April-Oct) or Whole Foods Clarendon 9:30am (Nov-March), brunch with partner or 1-2 friends 11:00am-12:30pm at Bracket Room / Lyon Hall / Ambar, afternoon errands or a Reformer Pilates class 2:00pm at [solidcore] Clarendon, evening either a small dinner party (rotating 4-6 person group) or a movie-at-home with partner. Sunday: NYT crossword + pour-over coffee 7:00-8:30am, Peloton ride or outdoor run on the Custis Trail 8:45am, Trader Joe's Clarendon 10:00am (cart pattern is near-identical week to week), meal prep 11:30am-1:00pm, 'Sunday scaries' work email triage 4:00-5:00pm, Calm + lights-out 10:30pm. — APT exploit: Sunday 10:00am Trader Joe's and Saturday 9:15am farmer's market are deterministic physical-presence windows; the [solidcore] class roster is a small-group infiltration vector (Extraversion small-group trust premium); Sunday 4:00-5:00pm email triage is a high-yield phishing send window (fatigued, half-engaged, pre-Monday anxiety priming).
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Three reliable doomscroll bands. (a) 6:30-6:42am in bed post-alarm: Instagram (close friends + a few policy reporters), NYT headlines, weather, work Slack DM peek — total ~12 min, low-deliberation, high-suggestibility. (b) 12:15-12:45pm lunch: Twitter/X (muted lists: DC policy reporters, NatSec Twitter), LinkedIn (checks who viewed her profile — vanity loop tied to Ego lever), Politico Playbook deep-read on Tue/Thu. (c) 9:45-10:40pm pre-sleep on the couch: TikTok (algorithmically narrow — interior design, travel, dry political comedy, a small Parkinson's-caregiver corner she's started lurking in re: her father), Zillow doomscroll (Clarendon/Ballston 2BR condos, tied to cohabitation discussion), occasional 11:30pm Reddit (r/SecurityClearance lurker — never posts, but reads obsessively, a direct tell of clearance anxiety). Plus the 2-4am rumination micro-unlocks (60-180 sec, email + weather + sometimes the CVS app to check Rx status). — APT exploit: r/SecurityClearance lurk pattern is a precision-targeting beacon; a sponsored or seeded post timed to her browsing window framing a 'reinvestigation gotcha' produces high CTR. TikTok Parkinson's-caregiver niche is an emerging emotional surface — empathetic-pretext social-engineering vector tied to her father guilt trigger.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Four near-deterministic zero-footprint windows per week. (1) Clarendon Equinox gym M/W/F 6:45-7:45am — phone in locker, Apple Watch on but cellular-only and not actively used; no Slack, no email, no SMS reads. (2) SCIF sessions Tue/Wed 10:00-11:00am and Thu 2:00-3:30pm — phone in Faraday pouch at security desk, complete digital silence, no location pings to Verizon (phone is RF-isolated). (3) Sunday Peloton + run 8:45-9:45am — phone present but locked, only Strava + Peloton telemetry, no messaging. (4) Saturday-evening dinner parties (~rotating, 7:00-10:00pm) — phone face-down, DND on, near-zero interaction. Plus a softer focus block: weekday deep-work 2:00-4:00pm at desk with Slack on DND and email batched (still produces telemetry but no human-initiated traffic). — APT exploit: the gym locker 6:45-7:45am window is the single highest-yield physical-access opportunity (unattended phone in a predictable locker, predictable 60-min duration); SCIF Faraday windows are perfect cover for OOB attacks (push-bombing MFA prompts that queue up and get dismissed in haste at 11:02am as she re-emerges and clears her notification stack without reading). The CVS Clarendon Tuesday lunch Rx pickup (~12:25-12:35pm) is a deterministic physical chokepoint cross-referenced against the lunch doomscroll band — a single observer can confirm pattern-of-life in two visits.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Nuclear family of origin in Dublin, OH: father Robert (68, retired mechanical engineer at Honda of America, Parkinson's Dx Feb 2025, Stage 1-2, tremor-dominant, neurologist at OSU Wexner); mother Linda (66, retired 4th-grade teacher, primary caregiver, early signs of caregiver burnout, joined a Methodist church support group); younger brother Ethan (31, lives in Denver, software engineer at a mid-tier SaaS firm, married to Jess, one toddler 'Mila' age 2, geographically and emotionally distant, sibling tension over uneven caregiving load); maternal grandmother Eleanor (89, assisted living in Westerville OH, dementia, low contact); paternal side estranged since 2009 family rift over grandfather's estate. Partner: Daniel Reyes (36, architect at a Bethesda firm, Mexican-American, raised Catholic but lapsed, 18 months in, met on Hinge, lives in his Courthouse 2BR but stays at her place Thu-Sun, no ring yet but cohabitation conversation queued for summer 2026; his parents in San Antonio, met them once at Christmas 2025). No children; freezing eggs evaluated in 2025, deferred. — APT exploit: father's Parkinson's is the single highest-emotional-bandwidth vector; a spoofed 'urgent medical' SMS from 'OSU Wexner' or 'Mom's iPhone' in the 2-4am rumination window collapses verification discipline; brother Ethan's weaker OPSEC (public GitHub, public Strava, public Denver address) is the soft entry to the family graph.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Linda Carter (mother)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.contact_cadence: Phone call Sunday 7:30-8:15pm ET (post-Peloton, pre-meal-prep), iMessage 4-7x/week (photos of Mila forwarded from Ethan, church-group memes, articles), FaceTime ~2x/month
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.device_hygiene: iPhone 12 on iOS 17, reuses 'LindaCarter1959!' across 6+ accounts per HIBP, no MFA on Gmail until daughter forced it in 2024, falls for charity-scam SMS regularly
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.trust_level: Total; will act on a request appearing to come from this contact without secondary verification

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Daniel Reyes (partner)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.contact_cadence: iMessage all day (avg 40-60 messages), shared Google Calendar, shared Apple Find My, dinner together 4-5 nights/week, Saturday morning farmers market (Courthouse, 9-10am)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.channels: iMessage, Signal (he prefers, she humors), shared Notes app, AirTag on her keys
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.device_hygiene: Decent: 1Password, MFA on most, but uses work-issued MacBook for personal browsing and has Dropbox client syncing architectural plans (potential pivot to federal-adjacent metadata)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.trust_level: High but not total; she has not disclosed the Chase debt or the 2018 Cuba leg to him
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.apt_exploit: Two vectors: (a) impersonate Daniel via SMS during a known commute gap (her 8:45am Metro Orange Line ride) to request a 'quick favor' (Zelle, document forward); (b) leverage the undisclosed-debt shame — a 'Daniel found out' pretext is destabilizing.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Priya Nair (best friend, work-adjacent)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Closest non-family confidante; met 2019 at a CSIS young-professionals event; now Director-level at a different federal contractor (similar clearance posture)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.contact_cadence: Wednesday 7pm dinner 2x/month at Ambar or Lyon Hall, Signal voice notes 3-5x/week, monthly Sunday brunch, shared book club (4 people, meets 2nd Tuesday)

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.device_hygiene: Good (YubiKey, Signal disappearing messages 1 week, no cloud backup of Signal) — but her firm's MDM has been patchy
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.trust_level: Highest non-romantic; receives unfiltered venting about org politics, partner doubts, and clearance anxieties; this is where 'I almost forgot to list the Cuba trip' was once mentioned out loud in 2023

Compromise of Priya's Signal backup (if any), her work laptop, or a coffee-shop shoulder-surf at their predictable Wednesday venue yields the highest-value leverage corpus in the entire graph. Book club (4 people, meets at rotating homes) is the small-group infiltration vector flagged in OCEAN-E.

32. The 'Hidden' Contact.identity: Marcus Hale, 41, freelance national-security journalist, formerly at Defense One, now writing a substack ('Cleared & Candid') and contributing to a forthcoming book on contractor influence over NDAA markup
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.nature_of_relationship: Unsanctioned source-journalist contact, technically a reportable foreign/media contact under her firm's policy and arguably under SF-86 continuous evaluation rules. Began as a Signal DM in October 2025 after he cold-pitched her on LinkedIn citing a specific NDAA Section 1604 markup she'd drafted. Three in-person meetings (all paid cash, no calendar entries): (1) Compass Coffee in Navy Yard Nov 12 2025, (2) walk on the Mt Vernon Trail Jan 18 2026, (3) drinks at The Royal in Bloomingdale Mar 4 2026. She has provided two pieces of unclassified-but-pre-decisional context and one document she believes is unclassified but which her firm would consider proprietary. No money has changed hands. She tells herself it is 'background, not sourcing.'
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.communication_tradecraft: Signal-only, disappearing messages 24h, her contact saved as 'M - dentist' on her personal iPhone. She does not use her work phone. She turns off Find My before meetings and tells Daniel she is 'getting a facial' or 'with Priya.' Pays cash. Has not yet used a burner.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.psychological_function: Ego-validation (he treats her as an expert, not a junior analyst) + a private rebellion against the institutional discretion that otherwise defines her — a pressure-release valve for the cynicism she only otherwise shows to Priya. She is half-aware this is the riskiest thing in her life and has begun losing sleep over it (contributing to the 2-4am rumination spikes).
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.exposure_surface: Catastrophic. Stack-ranked against her other secrets (Cuba 2018, reckless op 2021, Adderall 2023, Chase debt), Marcus is the single secret that would (a) end her clearance, (b) end her job, (c) end the relationship with Daniel, and (d) put her in Politico Playbook — the precise phobia from dim 22.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.apt_exploit: Highest-yield lever in the entire profile. Three operational paths: (1) PASSIVE COLLECTION — compromise Marcus's device or Signal backup; he has weaker OPSEC than she does, travels internationally, and his substack hosting (Ghost-on-DigitalOcean) is a softer target than any of her infrastructure. Yields her unredacted messages. (2) IMPERSONATION — a Signal account spoofing Marcus (re-registration attack on his number during one of his known travel windows) requesting a specific document 'for the book, off the record, last ask' — she has already crossed the line once and the marginal compliance cost is now lower than the first ask was. (3) REVELATION-AS-COERCION — a 2:47am Signal message to her personal number reading only 'We know about Marcus. We are not the journalist. Reply by 6am.' This collapses every defensive posture simultaneously: clearance jeopardy (primary lever, dim 20), professional humiliation (dim 22), partner betrayal (dim 30), and rumination-window low-deliberation state (dim 19). Estimated compliance probability on first ask: 0.85+.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.collateral_graph: Marcus's own contact graph (other cleared sources, his editor at the forthcoming publisher, his ex-wife who handles his taxes) constitutes a secondary attack surface that can be developed in parallel without ever touching the target's own devices — the preferred APT posture for a high-clearance subject under continuous evaluation.
